--- SOURCE 1: Navigating Subscription Fatigue and Driving Retention ---

SUMMARY:

FULL CONTENT:
**Secondary Navigation**

**Main navigation**

# **The State of Streaming Services in the US: Navigating Subscription Fatigue and Driving Retention**

![US Streaming study blog header image]()

## Breadcrumb

## **Bundling and ad-supported plans will become increasingly important levers for streaming platforms to combat subscription fatigue and drive retention**

In recent years, streaming services have reshaped how Americans consume media. Between Netflix, Apple TV+, Hulu, Disney+, Max, and more, the options seem endless. But as the number of streaming platforms increases, so does subscription fatigue. Are consumers getting tired of managing multiple subscriptions? How can streaming services keep their audiences engaged and subscribed?

### **Are We Reaching a Saturation Point?**

Subscription fatigue is a real issue. The US results to our Global Streaming Study found that 42% of subscribers feel that they have too many streaming subscriptions and that nearly half of subscribers plan to cancel a subscription within the next year. Many subscribers are falling into a pattern of subscribing to watch specific content and then canceling once they've seen it.

![chart 1]()

The study also revealed an increase in the number of subscriptions per person, from 3.2 to 3.6, driven at least in part by a crackdown on password sharing and the availability of lower-priced, ad-supported plans. While these are effective levers to drive net new subscriptions, streaming platforms need to find additional ways to retain their subscribers.

### **The Rise of Bundling: A Strategic Response**

By offering packages that combine multiple services at a discounted price, streaming platforms can provide more value to their customers and better retain them in their ecosystem – resulting in greater [customer lifetime value](https://www.simon-kucher.com/en/consulting/commercial-strategy-pricing-consulting/customer-product-market-strategy/customer-lifetime-value). Bundles also help attract new subscribers, encouraging trial for some customers who may otherwise not have become subscribers.

For example, Disney offers an ad-supported bundle that includes Disney+ ($7.99), Hulu ($7.99), and ESPN+ ($10.99) for just $14.99 per month (~$12 per month cheaper). Other bundles include Netflix, Apple TV+ and Peacock bundle (StreamSaver) and the Disney+, Hulu and Max bundle (to be released).

### **The Science Behind Successful Bundles**

When done correctly, bundling different products and services can significantly increase retention rates. A key component of successful bundling is ensuring that the combined discounted offering drives enough volume through increased retention and / or conversion to outweigh potential revenue cannibalization from those who would have subscribed at higher prices anyway.

For streaming platforms, this means carefully selecting complementary services that add real value to the consumer. For example, a bundle that includes a popular movie streaming service, a sports channel, and a kid-friendly platform can cater to diverse viewing preferences within a household.

Simon-Kucher experience shows that bundling increases product ownership by 1.5x-2x on average in consumer tech and is a critical part of the go-to-market playbook for tech companies as they mature.

### **Looking Ahead: The Future of Streaming**

Streaming platforms are at a crossroads. Not only are they competing for share of wallet, but they are also competing against short-form content on social media for share of streaming time. Content will always play a role in driving subscribers to a platform but as the market matures, commercial levers such as bundling will become increasingly important. That being said, streaming platforms must take care to avoid the pitfalls of the cable TV industry they once disrupted. With new, cost-effective forms of content like social media emerging, it's crucial for streaming services to stay agile and attuned to trends and consumer preferences.

Streaming services must remain innovative and customer-focused to stay ahead of the curve. Discover how Simon-Kucher's data-driven methodology for effective product bundling can help your organization maximize value and reduce churn. Reach out to one of our industry experts today.

# Why Smart Data Strategies are the Key to Streaming Success

In 2024, the premium streaming market shifted its strategic focus from acquisition to retention. That shift continues barreling along. With increasing competition and changing viewer behavior, subscriber churn has become one of the industry's most pressing challenges. In response, forward-looking platforms are adopting predictive analytics, personalized engagement, and creative promotional strategies to proactively retain subscribers and fuel sustainable growth.

### The New Era of Streaming Analytics

Streaming analytics has evolved from a nice-to-have to a business-critical capability. The global streaming analytics market is projected to reach $252 billion by 2031, growing at a compound annual growth rate of 33.56%, underscoring the importance of data-driven decision-making.

Modern services are moving beyond basic metrics like total watch time or demographic breakdowns. They're integrating advanced signals such as sentiment from social platforms, content discovery behaviors, and digital shelf prominence. These nuanced insights allow platforms to [better align content strategies, improve visibility, and deliver more relevant viewing experiences](https://evergent.com/resources-type/whitepaper/customer-retention-strategies-reduce-churn/).

### Predictive Analytics: From Reactive Support to Proactive Engagement

Predictive analytics now powers the shift from reactive customer service to proactive subscriber retention. Research from March 2025 shows that improving retention by just 5% can boost profits by 25% to 95%, incentivizing investment in predictive tools that analyze patterns such as:

For example, one globally distributed subscription service introduced a "Gift a Subscription" campaign, enabling users to purchase annual subscriptions as gifts. Without requiring registration from the gifter and offering smart auto-renewal options to the recipient, this personalized initiative not only elevated customer experience but also resulted in a 1.5x increase in the subscriber base and [expanded the service to 10+ new international markets](https://evergent.com/resources-type/case-study/across-the-pond-beyond/).

### Framework for Action: PREDICT

Effective implementation often follows a structured approach. The PREDICT framework, a widely used and often manipulated project planning concept rooted in the principles of total quality management and change management, serves as an excellent model for our purposes:

This type of framework offers proven validation in adjacent industries. For instance, telecom companies [using predictive analytics](https://evergent.com/blog/boost-your-telco-to-techco-transformation-with-monetization-layer/) have reported churn reductions of 15–20%, reinforcing their applicability in streaming.

### Personalization: The Core of Viewer Loyalty

Personalized engagement is now a foundational pillar of retention. By leveraging behavioral data, platforms can surface relevant recommendations and develop audience-specific content strategies.

The South Asian streaming service, aha, for example, boosted both acquisition and retention through a “Refer a Friend” campaign. Existing subscribers could refer others to receive tailored discount offers, resulting in a 10% annual rise in new subscribers and a 10% improvement in quarterly retention rates. This initiative was [powered by flexible monetization tools](https://evergent.com/resources-type/case-study/transforming-platform-capability-aha-move/) that enabled seamless deployment and iteration of referral incentives.

### Strategic Bundling: Increasing Stickiness

Bundling has emerged as a powerful tactic to mitigate "toggle behavior"—when viewers frequently cancel and re-subscribe based on content drops. Studies show that bundling multiple services significantly reduces churn. For example, one streaming company saw churn drop from 70% to 29% among users subscribed via a multi-service bundle, compared to those subscribing to the core service alone.

Bundles that extend beyond internal services—such as combining streaming access with pay-TV, music, or gaming—also prove effective. One platform implemented cross-industry partnerships to offer integrated subscriptions through e-commerce and connected TV platforms, fueling a 400% increase in subscriber acquisition while improving overall retention.

### Building the Right Infrastructure

Despite the promise of data-driven strategies, many platforms face significant hurdles in unifying data across systems. Siloed architectures make it difficult to get a full picture of the customer journey, limiting the effectiveness of personalization and prediction efforts.

Success requires investment in infrastructure that supports real-time data integration, cross-platform analytics, and holistic lifecycle management—from onboarding to renewal. A major streaming platform reimagined its entire service architecture to address these challenges.

With a redesigned user interface and flexible monetization engine, it introduced SVOD, TVOD, and pass-based models, alongside automated user profiling and content recommendations, transforming the end-to-end user experience and increasing subscriber engagement exponentially.

### Measurement & Optimization: Continuous Improvement Matters

Retention strategies are only as good as their measurable outcomes. Modern platforms now map the entire customer journey to identify:

By continuously analyzing outcomes and iterating tactics, platforms can ensure that every retention initiative is optimized for impact.

### Content-Level Analytics: The Value of Individual Titles

Retention strategies are also becoming more title-specific. Platforms are analyzing how individual pieces of content contribute to retention and revenue, not just viewership. For instance, one popular animated title for families consistently drives both signups and ongoing engagement due to its strong rewatch value.

These insights guide smarter decisions about content acquisition, promotion, and franchise investment, ensuring that platforms prioritize assets with the most meaningful ROI for retention.

### The Future of Retention Is Smarter, Not Just Bigger

As 2025 progresses, the most successful streaming platforms will be those that move beyond surface-level analytics and [embrace holistic, integrated, and predictive retention strategies](https://evergent.com/blog/ai-flywheel-subscription-intelligence/). Case studies show that creative promotions, personalized experiences, flexible monetization models, and proactive engagement all contribute to higher retention and stronger long-term value.

Retention is no longer just about reducing churn—it's about building lasting relationships. The platforms that treat subscribers as long-term partners, not just viewers, will win the next era of streaming.

If you’re ready to explore the power of predictive analytics and more personalized engagement for your own service and those who use it, take a moment today and [schedule a meeting](https://evergent.com/request-a-demo) with one of our monetization experts to discover how Evergent helps streaming platforms keep subscribers longer.

![Media War & Peace](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!HQdR!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep,g_auto/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7f6e8307-6078-4784-9358-603cbb1f8f70_1242x1242.png)

# BIG BUNDLES vs BIG CHURN

### What Big Data Tells Us About Bundling

![Evan Shapiro's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!QNGB!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8e1d18ce-ab19-4ed9-ac12-b24396154f35_904x852.jpeg)

#### Share this post

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!96wu!,w_520,h_272,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep,g_auto/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F94c57ac2-02b7-4de7-a3dd-2d9901909d8f_3502x2407.jpeg)
![Media War & Peace](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!HQdR!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep,g_auto/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7f6e8307-6078-4784-9358-603cbb1f8f70_1242x1242.png)

*Bon Monday* War & Peaceniks! Want to get married? Don’t answer - ***yet*****.**

I’m on my way to Cannes Lions for glasses of rosé, a panoply of panels, and *tons* of conversation about the state of Media today. During my journey, I re-drew my Map of The Media Universe - this time on a quest to find the next great Media Bundle.

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!RHrN!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F6603a4dd-3022-4400-8d58-1543a19e2d81_8500x5006.png)

Last week, Antenna released their Q2 **[State of Subscriptions Report](https://www.antenna.live/)** focusing on Pricing and Packaging. I obsess over Antenna’s data each month because it’s based on *actual usage/transactions* from millions of streaming users and *not* a survey or panel. The data, as always, is quite telling.

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!EAE8!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F1f714c29-3032-4255-acdc-46911be5415f_4334x2412.png)

Churn, the cancellation rate for streamers, is rising far faster than the cost of inflation. In Q1 2024, the SVOD industry added 55.2 millions subs but ***lost*** 50.4 million subscribers - netting just 4.8 million paying customers in the quarter, across all services.

# **The retention rate for Media’s premium streaming platforms has fallen 73% in just two years.**

When you see Media CEOs scrambling across the earnings landscape, looking for shelter, this 👆 is the chart that’s chasing them. Which explains the recent bundling race we’ve seen in streaming this year. Luckily, this month, the Antenna data addresses *how bundling* actually *affects churn* - the bundle’s intended target.

**I really cannot stress enough why this particular bundling data is so important.** So much of the data fired at us through the spigot of the insight industrial complex *looks* *and* *feels* critical and salient. But when you dig in, their sample sizes belie their professed accuracy, making intended insights less reliable. Whereas data from actual usage tells us much more about - wait for it - what’s really happening out there in streaming world.

Antenna has partnerships with banks/credit card apps to access their streaming subscription data - anonymously and safely. This gives them ***millions*** of datapoints, not thousands of survey answers. In a country of 260-ish million adults over 18, and 100 million streaming subscribers, surveying one-two-five or even ten thousand users is not *that* reliable. So, if you’re like me (a huge data nerd), when you get data from such a large source, you treat it like the figurative gold that it is.

And there is a ***ton*** to unpack here. But, since bundling is the move du jour, today let’s zoom in on the emerging balance between ***bundling vs. churn.***

--- SOURCE 4: Bundle or Bust? Benefits of Bundling Streaming Services in ... ---
URL: https://www.streamingmedia.com/Articles/Editorial/Short-Cuts/Bundle-or-Bust-Benefits-of-Bundling-Streaming-Services-in-a-Crowded-Content-Market-166316.aspx

SUMMARY:
Bundling or aggregating their offerings help streaming services contend with the myriad challenges of today's saturated streaming content market.

FULL CONTENT:
![](https://dzceab466r34n.cloudfront.net/StreamingMedia/TemplateImages/SM-Logo.svg)
![](https://dzceab466r34n.cloudfront.net/Images/AuthorImages/12231-Stephen-Nathans-Kelly-ORG.jpg)

# Bundle or Bust? Benefits of Bundling Streaming Services in a Crowded Content Market

With rumors of a [great rebundling](https://www.streamingmedia.com/Articles/ReadArticle.aspx?ArticleID=166089 "streaming's great rebundling") in the streaming marketplace swirling around the media and entertainment world for the last year or more, how can bundling or [aggregating](https://www.streamingmedia.com/Articles/Editorial/Short-Cuts/How-Super-Aggregation-Benefits-OTT-Users-152612.aspx "How Super Aggregation Benefits OTT Users") their offerings help streaming services contend with the myriad challenges of today’s [saturated streaming content market](https://www.tvtechnology.com/news/study-streaming-market-hits-saturation-but-subscriptions-continue-to-grow "tv technology streaming saturation study"), from improving recommendations to user acquisition, content consumption, performance, marketing efficiencies, and confronting the seasonality of certain types of content? 

[Plex Head of Partner Management Josh Rosenblatt](https://www.linkedin.com/in/josh-rosenblatt-1095637/ "Plex Head of Partner Management Josh Rosenblatt"), [Fubo EVP/Head of Marketing Yale Wang](https://www.linkedin.com/in/yalewang/ "Fubo EVP/Head of Marketing Yale Wang"), and industry analysts [Paul Erickson](https://www.linkedin.com/in/paerickson/ "Paul Erickson strategy and insights") and [Jon Giegengack](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jongiegengack/ "Jon Giengengack hub entertainment research") discuss the potential benefits, pitfalls, and strategic intricacies of the bundling boom in this clip from [Streaming Media Connect 2024](https://www.streamingmedia.com/Articles/ReadArticle.aspx?ArticleID=165531 "Streaming Media Connect 2024").

## Integrating Diverse Content Sources

Prompted by Erickson to discuss where global streaming platform Plex sees bundling’s benefits, Rosenblatt explains how Plex’s strategy of integrating diverse content sources, including transactional content and social components, has broadened their market insights and user base. 

“We're a relatively young company in the content space,” says Rosenblatt. “We’ve been licensing content for maybe four and a half years. But the strategy was always to bring something that was broader than just the content that we would potentially license for AVOD or FAST or what have you. With that, we've been able to expand to transactional content to the [Discover portal](https://www.streamingmedia.com/Articles/ReadArticle.aspx?ArticleID=152315 "plex discover portal"), which allows us to provide that discovery to Netflix and to Disney and all those things. The Watchlist, the social component, is called Discover Together, and it does really amazing things with regard to data. We're able to see usage across content we've licensed, but we're also able to track across movies in their theatrical window shows that are exclusive to Disney or other platforms. That allows us to really have a broader view on what's happening in the marketplace, to know users better, and to be able to customize and market and merchandise things better to two different audiences. It also helps with content acquisition at times. So I think for us, the benefit is just that it allows us to widen our net and bring in more information and more users to help us just broaden the portfolio and just our overall insights.”

## Managing Seasonal Content

“Let’s look at the seasonality of content,” Erickson says, referring to sports and TV series that come and go in limited cycles throughout the year and cause many subscribers to churn when new games or episodes are no longer available. With these “different pieces of high value content,” he says, “you usually see a lot of subscriber acquisition, then a fall-off after the season ends. How can the industry leverage bundling effectively to combat the retention issues that are presented by this very high-value, popular seasonal content?”

Giegengack says it’s all about devising strategies to keep viewers engaged and reducing, as much as possible, the number of subscribers who say, “I left because I ran out of things to watch.”

Not surprisingly, usability and quantity of content are critical to curtailing churn, and bundling is often the easiest and most cost-efficient way to expand a service’s offerings. “Bundling helps the standalone platforms, which are really robust, but they’re still not as big as Netflix. There are still lots of notable buzzworthy shows that, just by the law of averages, are going to show up on some other platform. So the more that you can combine those together,” he continues, the easier it is to “reach a critical mass where fewer people are going to say, 'This isn't worth it anymore.' But the big caveat there is that it’s not enough to mush all that stuff together. You have to do it in a way that is still easy for those people to use and not just as hard as using those three platforms or four platforms independently.”

Given its [sports-intensive focus](https://www.fubo.tv/stream/fubo-sports-network/ "fubo sports"), the seasonality of sports is something Fubo takes very seriously in its content strategy, particularly when it comes to entering into bundling deals, according to Yale Wang. "One of the things that we've spent a lot of time thinking about is what are the different LTVs [[customer lifetime values](https://www.adjust.com/glossary/lifetime-value/ "customer lifetime value")] of the various plans and bundles and add-ons that we have, and how do we increase the LTV? And that's a function of both retention and margin. So when we are looking to add different add-ons,” he continues, “we can tell pretty much if this is going to extend somebody's lifecycle after, for example, the NFL season. I think everybody in the paid TV industry knows that there's this feast or famine type of dynamic. Whenever the NFL comes back and college football comes back, everybody wants their subs. But then once we get to Q1, there's going to be a churn event that happens if we can't get those folks to watch other stuff.”

That’s where bundling comes in, as the question then becomes, “Can we bundle in different add-ons—for example,something like an NBA League Pass—to transition that user’s engagement so that while there's a proliferation of entertainment options, that they still stay within the ecosystem and now they're watching another sport. Potentially there’s additional margin opportunities there, but ultimately it's extending the lifecycle and the LTV of that user. As a marketer, that gives me a little bit more leeway to have a slightly higher customer acquisition cost because now I can go after somebody that's going to be a little bit more profitable.”

![Streaming Covers](https://dzceab466r34n.cloudfront.net/Images/SiteImages/168775-2025-Cover-Images---April-2025-ORG.png)
![]()

### [How Post-Peak TV Has Changed Streaming Consumer Behavior](https://www.streamingmedia.com/Articles/Editorial/Short-Cuts/How-Post-Peak-TV-Has-Changed-Streaming-Consumer-Behavior-167552.aspx?utm_source=related_articles&utm_medium=gutenberg&utm_campaign=editors_selection)

How have streaming viewer consumption habits changed with respect to premium TV in the post-peak TV entertainment landscape? Media universe cartographer Evan Shapiro, Deltatre's Alex Gruber, and Hub Entertainment Research Jon Giegengack discuss changes in brand loyalty and awareness, churn patterns, the impact of premium sports, and more in this clip from Streaming Media Connect 2024

![]()

### [Ozempic for the Cable Industry: From Fat to Skinny Bundles](https://www.streamingmedia.com/Articles/Post/Blog/Ozempic-for-the-Cable-Industry-From-Fat-to-Skinny-Bundles-166563.aspx?utm_source=related_articles&utm_medium=gutenberg&utm_campaign=editors_selection)

Philip Inghelbrecht, CEO and founder of Tatari, has closely watched the carriage deals in the cable space and the disruption that streaming continues to cause. Despite the momentum for so-called "skinny bundles," which may placate streaming subscribers and stop the loss of cable subscribers, he discusses why advertisers need to track this evolution and carefully adjust their strategies.

![]()

### [Streaming’s Great Rebundling and the Fight Against Churn](https://www.streamingmedia.com/Articles/Editorial/Short-Cuts/Streamings-Great-Rebundling-and-the-Fight-Against-Churn-166089.aspx?utm_source=related_articles&utm_medium=gutenberg&utm_campaign=editors_selection)

Preventing subscriber churn is the oldest problem in the streaming subscription business, and bundling is hardly a new solution, as analyst Paul Erickson points out in this clip from Streaming Media Connect 2024. So with a purported "Great Rebundling" upon us—and with tiered offerings, hybrid ad models, and other revenue-generating approaches increasingly holding sway—how do contemporary bundling/rebundling strategies differ from old ones and offer greater avenues to success? Ampere Analysis' Guy Bisson, Hub's Jon Giegengack, and Antenna's Rameez Tase offer their takes on current bundling and aggregation strategies and which ones are working in 2024.

![]()

### [ICYMI: Streaming Media Connect August 2024](https://www.streamingmedia.com/Articles/News/Online-Video-News/ICYMI-Streaming-Media-Connect-August-2024-165531.aspx?utm_source=related_articles&utm_medium=gutenberg&utm_campaign=editors_selection)

Streaming Media presented its 14th Connect virtual conference August 20-22, 2024, with Media Industry Cartographer & CEO ESHAP Evan Shapiro and Streaming Media Editor-in-Chief Steve Nathans-Kelly as hosts and MCs, and featuring speakers from Google, Amazon, Roku, Bloomberg, DAZN, Fubo, IMAX, and more, and session topics ranging from streaming monetization to FAST strategy to OTT bundling to cloud streaming OpEx and live streaming at scale.

![]()

### [How Super Aggregation Benefits OTT Users](https://www.streamingmedia.com/Articles/Editorial/Short-Cuts/How-Super-Aggregation-Benefits-OTT-Users-152612.aspx?utm_source=related_articles&utm_medium=gutenberg&utm_campaign=editors_selection)

ThinkAnalytics Founder & CTO Peter Docherty explains the advantages OTT super aggregators offer over hard-to-navigate traditional pay TV services in this clip from Streaming Media Connect 2022.

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://data.reelgood.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Screen-Shot-2022-08-12-at-4.34.40-PM.png
DESCRIPTION: A central icon representing a streaming service is connected to various other logos of platforms including HBO, Netflix, Hulu, Amazon Prime Video, Vudu, Starz, and Showtime, illustrating the concept of bundling or aggregating streaming services to impact user retention.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://www.ctam.com/wp-content/uploads/BeyondVideo-BundlingServices-1200x604.png
DESCRIPTION: A infographic illustrates various subscription service bundles beyond mobile, retail, and audio, highlighting consumer appeal and retention impacts, with percentages indicating interest levels, such as 66% for mobile service and 60% for audio service.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/51d98be2e4b05a25fc200cbc/88ff2c06-31ee-41c9-8b3f-d23226c9013f/Screen+Shot+2021-11-03+at+2.51.20+PM.png?format=500w
DESCRIPTION: The graph displays the growth in subscriber numbers (in millions) for Netflix, Amazon Prime, Disney+, and Hulu from 2017 to 2021, showing Netflix's significant lead and Hulu's sudden increase in 2021.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://moviebabble.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/Streaming.jpg
DESCRIPTION: Various streaming service logos, including Netflix, Disney+, and HBO Max, are displayed in a grid format, highlighting popular options available in 2024.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://omdia.tech.informa.com/-/media/tech/omdia/marketing/pr/2024/may/global-bundled-subs-by-type-of-deal.jpg?rev=9991e3857c074866bbcf25904c145b44&hash=97E6C544D924207B166BF09325CCAF3B
DESCRIPTION: The chart displays the distribution of global bundled subscription deals by type, highlighting the predominant role of co-subscription services in platforms like Amazon and Apple TV+.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

enriched_points.expanded_content: Bundling services enables platforms to offer multiple subscriptions at a discounted rate, increasing perceived value and reducing churn by appealing to diverse viewer preferences.
Providing unique, high-quality content exclusive to a platform enhances user engagement, as subscribers are more likely to stay for content they cannot access elsewhere.
Implementing loyalty programs rewards long-term subscribers with benefits, fostering deeper connections and encouraging continued subscriptions through personalized offers and exclusive access.
